plot_hooks.title: Seen Through Your Eyes
plot_hooks.setup: During a relatively calm night in Elysium or at the Violet Reliquary, Lilith suddenly fixes "that" look on a PC and falls briefly into a vision.
plot_hooks.hook: Lilith describes in unnerving detail a small but specific moment the PC has not yet experienced — a stranger’s voice behind them, a broken watch they haven’t found, or a betrayal they haven’t committed.
objectives: Understand what future moment Lilith is describing and where it might occur.
Decide whether to avoid, delay, or deliberately fulfill the future she has seen.
complications: Other NPCs overhear parts of the prophecy and try to manipulate the PC into fulfilling or preventing it.
The PC’s attempts to avoid the vision unintentionally move them closer to making it happen.
potential_outcomes: The PC fulfills the vision exactly, gaining eerie trust in Lilith’s powers.
The PC alters the circumstances but cannot escape the core event.
The PC appears to avert the vision… only to realize it manifested in a different, darker way.
plot_hooks.opportunity: A character-defining choice about whether the PC embraces or resists a foretold future.
plot_hooks.value: Introduces Lilith as a personal oracle and establishes the tone of visions in the chronicle.
twist_options: The vision was actually borrowed from another Malkavian’s perspective.
The vision was focused on a different character, but the PC misinterpreted it.
plot_hooks.emotional_stakes: The PC feels singled out by the universe — or by the Madness Network — and must decide what that means for their identity.
plot_hooks.character_moment: Lilith shows a rare, fragile concern for how much future she should share.
plot_hooks.escalation: Subsequent visions about the same PC become sharper, more specific, and harder to ignore.
plot_hooks.twist: The vision’s true subject is not the PC, but someone they will come to care about.
hook_options: Lilith singles out a PC the first time they meet.
The vision occurs after the PC has already heard rumors about her accuracy.
plot_hooks.pc_involvement: The PC is the center of the vision and must decide how literally to treat Lilith’s words.
plot_hooks.stakes: Success gives the PC more control over the outcome; failure deepens their sense of being a pawn in someone else’s story.
plot_hooks.deeper_layer: The Madness Network may be using Lilith to place the PC into a specific role in the city’s future.
plot_hooks.the_task: Track the symbols, people, and locations that match Lilith’s description and choose how to act when they appear.
task_examples: Recognize the stranger’s voice in a crowded club.
Notice a broken watch in a crime scene.
Realize the small betrayal moment before deciding whether to commit it.
plot_hooks.complication: Lilith herself is uncertain whether the vision is fixed or still malleable.
options: Lean into the vision to exploit it.
plot_hooks.investigation: Follow symbolic clues from the vision to real-world locations or NPCs.

act_i.name: The Vision
plays: Lilith experiences the vision and publicly or privately addresses the PC.
Other Kindred react with unease or interest in the prophecy.

act_ii.name: Chasing Signs
plays: The PC encounters fragments of the foretold moment.
Choices alter the timing or context of the coming event.

act_iii.name: The Moment Arrives
plays: The vision manifests in some form.
The PC deals with the emotional and political fallout.

failure_points: The PC ignores the prophecy and is blindsided by the event.
The PC overcorrects and causes collateral damage trying to avoid it.
resources: Other Malkavians who might have seen adjacent visions.

plot_hooks.title: The Glass Shatter Moment
plot_hooks.setup: Lilith suffers a violent vision in Elysium or her haven: hands not her own, a crystal goblet, shattering glass, and blood.
plot_hooks.hook: She asks the PCs to help identify whose perspective she borrowed and where the scene takes place before it actually happens.
objectives: Identify the location glimpsed in the vision from environmental details.
Determine whose hands were holding the glass and why blood was present.
Prevent or exploit the coming event tied to the Prince’s death or another assassination.
complications: Multiple locations in Phoenix match the vague description.
Several NPCs use crystal goblets in Elysium, implicating high-status Kindred.
themes: Trust and suspicion in the Court
potential_outcomes: The PCs arrive in time to prevent a killing, changing the political board.
The vision plays out exactly, but the PCs gain valuable evidence.
The PCs misidentify the location and someone else dies in the “wrong” place.
plot_hooks.opportunity: Connect Lilith’s visions directly to the Prince’s murder arc or a major assassination attempt.
plot_hooks.value: Gives the PCs an investigative role with real stakes in Court politics.
twist_options: The vision is of the past, not the future, revealing a covered-up crime.
The blood in the vision belongs to a PC, not the eventual victim.
plot_hooks.emotional_stakes: If they fail, the PCs must live with the knowledge that they saw the warning and still couldn’t change it.
plot_hooks.character_moment: Lilith visibly struggles with whether to share all she saw, implying personal cost for carrying these images.
plot_hooks.escalation: Subsequent visions show clearer angles or new participants, pulling the PCs deeper.
plot_hooks.twist: The glass shattering is a signal, not an accident — part of a prearranged code between conspirators.
hook_options: Lilith collapses mid-conversation with a PC.
Her vision interrupts a formal Court announcement.
plot_hooks.pc_involvement: PCs become front-line investigators who must interpret symbolic psychic evidence.
plot_hooks.stakes: Success prevents or redirects a killing; failure costs a major NPC or accelerates city-wide chaos.
role_for_pcs: Potential scapegoats if things go wrong
plot_hooks.deeper_layer: Certain factions may want the PCs to misinterpret the vision, feeding them false “confirmations.”
plot_hooks.the_task: Match vision details (furnishings, music, view from windows) to real Phoenix locations.
task_examples: Look for a location where the ambient sound matches the vision.
plot_hooks.complication: Every NPC the PCs question now knows Lilith is having specific visions about murder.
options: Leak parts of the vision to pit factions against each other.
plot_hooks.investigation: Standard street/Influence investigation plus Auspex-powered interviews and cross-referencing Lilith’s symbolic cues.

act_i.name: The Shattering Vision
plays: Lilith experiences the vision and shares it with the PCs.
The PCs gather the initial set of symbolic details.

act_ii.name: Hunting the Scene
plays: The PCs check likely locations and suspects.
False leads and faction interference slow progress.

act_iii.name: When the Glass Breaks
plays: The scene from the vision unfolds.
PCs intervene or witness, reshaping the narrative.

failure_points: Arriving too late to save the victim.
Triggering political backlash by accusing the wrong person.
resources: Nosferatu or Tremere willing to quietly help verify details.

usage_notes.scaling: Start with smaller, personal hooks like 'Seen Through Your Eyes' to introduce Lilith’s tone and cost, then move into city-shaping hooks as PCs gain influence.

difficulty_guide.Medium: Hooks that impact local politics and key NPCs.
difficulty_guide.Hard: Hooks tied to Prince’s death and Madness Network stability.

usage_notes.combination: Run one personal hook in parallel with one investigative hook to keep Lilith relevant without dominating every scene.
usage_notes.character_growth: These hooks push PCs to decide how much they trust prophecy and whether they will protect or exploit an oracle.
